info.title: CIROH National Water Model API
info.summary: This is the version 2.0.0 of the CIROH National Water Model API with new endpoints, derived datasets, and enhanced capabilities.
info.description: This API dedicatedly serves National Water Model (NWM) data leveraging the efficient retrieval from corresponding BigQuery datasets. It includes endpoints for accessing natively generated operational ('analyses-assim', 'forecasts') and retrospective ('retrospectives') datasets, derived data products ('flow-metrics', 'percentile-flows' and 'return-periods'), flowline geometry ('geometries') data, and reach-based data compilation. Beside reach identifier based extraction, this API also supports geospatial queries, allowing users to define spatial boundaries, location, or alternative identifiers for their data requests. Moreover, the API can extensively validate user inputs with customized error messages and can serve static and dynamic metadata. Please refer to the individual endpoint documentation for more details on how to use each of them to find your required NWM data.

get.description:
Access the forecast configuration data for one of the three forecast types at a certain reference time 
against one or more reaches from the National Water Model operational dataset either in geospatial
(geojson or geopackage) or structured (csv or json) format by filtering the data for one or more of 
the available ensemble members, as well as by filtering the reaches through geospatial location, extent, 
or direct identifier-based parameters.

parameters.name: reach_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Reach Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: gage_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Gage Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: hydroshare_id

schema.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.
schema.title: Hydroshare Id

parameters.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.

parameters.name: bounding_box

schema.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'
schema.title: Bounding Box

parameters.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'

parameters.name: geom_filter

schema.description: A string containing GeoJSON, WKT, or WKB representation of the geometry to filter within. Supported geometry types are: Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, and GeometryCollection.
schema.title: Geom Filter

parameters.description: A string containing GeoJSON, WKT, or WKB representation of the geometry to filter within. Supported geometry types are: Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, and GeometryCollection.

parameters.name: huc

schema.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.
schema.title: Huc

parameters.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.

parameters.name: lon

schema.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.
schema.title: Lon

parameters.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.

parameters.name: lat

schema.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.
schema.title: Lat

parameters.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.

parameters.name: with_buffer

schema.description: Buffer distance (0 to 1000 meters) to apply when filtering geometries.
schema.title: With Buffer

parameters.description: Buffer distance (0 to 1000 meters) to apply when filtering geometries.

parameters.name: lowest_stream_order

schema.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).
schema.title: Lowest Stream Order

parameters.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).

parameters.name: output_format

schema.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.
schema.title: Output Format

parameters.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.

parameters.name: ordered

schema.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.
schema.title: Ordered

parameters.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.

parameters.name: metadata

schema.description: Whether to embed metadata with the actual data.
schema.title: Metadata

parameters.description: Whether to embed metadata with the actual data.

parameters.name: time_zone

schema.description: The time zone for the provided time parameters and outputs. Default is 'UTC'.
schema.title: Time Zone

parameters.description: The time zone for the provided time parameters and outputs. Default is 'UTC'.

parameters.name: forecast_type

schema.description: The type of forecast to retrieve as a string.
schema.title: Forecast Type

parameters.description: The type of forecast to retrieve as a string.

parameters.name: reference_time

schema.description: The reference time of the forecast to retrieve preferably in ISO format (e.g. '2024-01-01T00:00:00'). If not provided, the most recent forecast will be retrieved.
schema.title: Reference Time

parameters.description: The reference time of the forecast to retrieve preferably in ISO format (e.g. '2024-01-01T00:00:00'). If not provided, the most recent forecast will be retrieved.

parameters.name: ensemble

schema.description: A string listing ensemble member(s), corresponding to the forecast type, to retrieve. Available ensemble member(s) are only 0 for short_range forecasts, are 0-5 (six) for medium_range forecasts, and are 0-3 (four) for long-range forecasts. If not provided, the average of all available ensemble members will be retrieved.
schema.title: Ensemble

parameters.description: A string listing ensemble member(s), corresponding to the forecast type, to retrieve. Available ensemble member(s) are only 0 for short_range forecasts, are 0-5 (six) for medium_range forecasts, and are 0-3 (four) for long-range forecasts. If not provided, the average of all available ensemble members will be retrieved.

get.description:
Access the analysis-assimilation simulation data for one or more of its offset runs against one or
more reaches from the National Water Model operational dataset either in geospatial (geojson or geopackage) 
or structured (csv or json) format by filtering the data based on time window and run offset,
as well as by filtering the reaches through geospatial location, extent, or direct identifier-based 
parameters.

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.

parameters.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.

schema.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'

parameters.name: lon

schema.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.
schema.title: Lon

parameters.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.

schema.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.

schema.title: With Buffer

parameters.name: lowest_stream_order

schema.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).
schema.title: Lowest Stream Order

parameters.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).

parameters.name: output_format

schema.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.
schema.title: Output Format

parameters.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.

parameters.name: ordered

schema.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.
schema.title: Ordered

schema.description: The time zone for the provided time parameters and outputs. Default is 'UTC'.

parameters.name: start_time

schema.description: The start time of the analysis-assimilation timeseries. If not provided, it will default to one month before the last timestep of the analysis run.
schema.title: Start Time

parameters.description: The start time of the analysis-assimilation timeseries. If not provided, it will default to one month before the last timestep of the analysis run.

parameters.name: end_time

schema.description: The end time of the analysis-assimilation timeseries. If not provided, it will default to the last timestep of the analysis run.
schema.title: End Time

parameters.description: The end time of the analysis-assimilation timeseries. If not provided, it will default to the last timestep of the analysis run.

parameters.name: run_offset

schema.description: A string listing offsets to include. Supported run offsets are: 1, 2, and 3, which correspond to the most recent run (offset 1), the second most recent run (offset 2), and the third most recent run (offset 3).
schema.title: Run Offset

parameters.description: A string listing offsets to include. Supported run offsets are: 1, 2, and 3, which correspond to the most recent run (offset 1), the second most recent run (offset 2), and the third most recent run (offset 3).

get.description:
Access the retrospective simulation data from its version 3.0 run of NOAA National Water Model,
against one or more reaches either in geospatial (geojson or geopackage) or structured (csv or json)
format by filtering the data based on time window (start and end time), as well as by filtering the 
reaches through geospatial location, extent, or direct identifier-based parameters.

parameters.name: reach_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.

schema.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'

parameters.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.

parameters.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).

schema.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.

get.description:
Access the streamflow metrics (or indices or indicators) data derived from the NOAA National Water Model
Retrospective 3.0 dataset, against one or more reaches either in geospatial (geojson or shapefile) or
structured (csv or json) format by filtering down to specific or all available metrics, as well as by
filtering the reaches through geospatial location, extent, or direct identifier-based parameters.

parameters.name: reach_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Reach Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: gage_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Gage Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: hydroshare_id

schema.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.
schema.title: Hydroshare Id

parameters.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.

parameters.name: bounding_box

schema.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'
schema.title: Bounding Box

parameters.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'

parameters.name: geom_filter

schema.description: A string containing GeoJSON, WKT, or WKB representation of the geometry to filter within. Supported geometry types are: Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, and GeometryCollection.
schema.title: Geom Filter

parameters.description: A string containing GeoJSON, WKT, or WKB representation of the geometry to filter within. Supported geometry types are: Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, and GeometryCollection.

parameters.name: huc

schema.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.
schema.title: Huc

parameters.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.

parameters.name: lon

schema.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.
schema.title: Lon

parameters.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.

parameters.name: lat

schema.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.
schema.title: Lat

parameters.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.

parameters.name: with_buffer

schema.description: Buffer distance (0 to 1000 meters) to apply when filtering geometries.
schema.title: With Buffer

parameters.description: Buffer distance (0 to 1000 meters) to apply when filtering geometries.

parameters.name: lowest_stream_order

schema.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).
schema.title: Lowest Stream Order

parameters.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).

parameters.name: output_format

schema.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.
schema.title: Output Format

parameters.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.

parameters.name: ordered

schema.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.
schema.title: Ordered

parameters.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.

parameters.name: metadata

parameters.name: metrics

schema.description: A comma-separated list of streamflow metrics to include. Supported metric names are: 'monthwise_mean','monthwise_cov','variability_index','slope_fdc','flashiness_index','sevenQ10','mean_annual_7_day_min','baseflow_index','zero_flow_days_n','low_flow_days_n','duration_low_flow_event','high_flow_days_n','duration_high_flow_event','half_flow_date', and 'start_date_flood_season'.
schema.title: Metrics

parameters.description: A comma-separated list of streamflow metrics to include. Supported metric names are: 'monthwise_mean','monthwise_cov','variability_index','slope_fdc','flashiness_index','sevenQ10','mean_annual_7_day_min','baseflow_index','zero_flow_days_n','low_flow_days_n','duration_low_flow_event','high_flow_days_n','duration_high_flow_event','half_flow_date', and 'start_date_flood_season'.

get.description:
Access the streamflow magnitude data corresponding to specified or all available percentiles, derived from 
the daily aggregation of the NOAA National Water Model Retrospective 3.0 dataset, against one or more reaches
either in geospatial (geojson or shapefile) or structured (csv or json) format by filtering the reaches through

get.description:
Access the flood return periods data of one or more reaches derived from the National Water Model
retrospective dataset either in geospatial (geojson or shapefile) or tabular (csv or json) format 
by filtering the return periods from the six available return periods (2, 5, 10, 25, 50, and 100 year)
as well as by filtering the reaches through geospatial location, extent, or direct identifier-based 
parameters.

parameters.name: reach_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Reach Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: gage_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Gage Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: hydroshare_id

schema.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.
schema.title: Hydroshare Id

parameters.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.

parameters.name: bounding_box

schema.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'
schema.title: Bounding Box

parameters.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'

parameters.name: geom_filter

schema.description: A string containing GeoJSON, WKT, or WKB representation of the geometry to filter within. Supported geometry types are: Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, and GeometryCollection.
schema.title: Geom Filter

parameters.description: A string containing GeoJSON, WKT, or WKB representation of the geometry to filter within. Supported geometry types are: Point, LineString, Polygon, MultiPoint, MultiLineString, MultiPolygon, and GeometryCollection.

parameters.name: huc

schema.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.
schema.title: Huc

parameters.description: A string of digits with length corresponding to valid HUC levels (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, or 12) representing the HUC code to filter data for the reaches within.

parameters.name: lon

schema.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.
schema.title: Lon

parameters.description: Longitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lat' parameter.

parameters.name: lat

schema.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.
schema.title: Lat

parameters.description: Latitude coordinate (as per WGS84) as a decimal degree to filter the reach nearby to this point. Must be provided together with 'lon' parameter.

parameters.name: with_buffer

schema.description: Buffer distance (0 to 1000 meters) to apply when filtering geometries.
schema.title: With Buffer

parameters.description: Buffer distance (0 to 1000 meters) to apply when filtering geometries.

parameters.name: lowest_stream_order

schema.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).
schema.title: Lowest Stream Order

parameters.description: The lowest stream order (1 to 10) for the reaches to include (e.g. '10' for tenth-order streams only, '9' for ninth and tenth-order streams only, '2' for second and all upper-order streams, etc.).

parameters.name: output_format

schema.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.
schema.title: Output Format

parameters.description: The output format of the response data. Supported formats are: 'geojson', 'shapefile', 'json', and 'csv'. Default is 'json'.

parameters.name: ordered

schema.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.
schema.title: Ordered

parameters.description: Whether to order the reaches in the output by relevant fields.

parameters.name: metadata

schema.description: Whether to embed metadata with the actual data.
schema.title: Metadata

parameters.description: Whether to embed metadata with the actual data.

parameters.name: return_periods

schema.description: A string containing combination of return periods: 2, 5, 10, 25, 50, and 100
schema.title: Return Periods

parameters.description: A string containing combination of return periods: 2, 5, 10, 25, 50, and 100

get.description:
Access the geometries data of one or more reaches from the National Water Model 
either in geospatial (geojson or shapefile) or structured (csv or json) format by filtering 
through geospatial location, extent, or direct identifier-based parameters.

parameters.name: reach_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Reach Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated National Water Model reach identifiers. Single reach_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: gage_id

schema.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.
schema.title: Gage Id

parameters.description: A string containing comma-separated USGS gage identifiers. Single gage_id is also allowed.

parameters.name: hydroshare_id

schema.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.
schema.title: Hydroshare Id

parameters.description: A 32-character hexadecimal string representing the HydroShare resource identifier containing a JSON file with specific filename and format.

parameters.name: bounding_box

schema.description: A string of comma-separated four coordinates (as per WGS84) representing the bounding box in the format: 'min_lon,min_lat,max_lon,max_lat'
schema.title: Bounding Box

parameters.name: geom_filter

get.description:
Access a compiled set of data for a specific reach identified by its NWM reach_id, in geojson format.
The compiled data includes the reach geometry and can include one, more, or all of streamflow metrics,
percentile flows, forecasts of different types, analysis-assimilation, and flood return periods data.
The data is retrieved against a time of reference to look ahead for forecasts and look back for 
analysis-assimilation data.

parameters.name: reach_id

schema.description: The reach identifier from the National Water Model or NHDFlowlines COMID from the NHDPlusV2 dataset.
schema.title: Reach Id

parameters.description: The reach identifier from the National Water Model or NHDFlowlines COMID from the NHDPlusV2 dataset.

parameters.name: include

schema.description: A comma-separated list of datasets to include in the response. Supported dataset options are: 'analyses_assim', 'forecasts_short_range', 'forecasts_medium_range', 'forecasts_long_range', 'return_periods', 'flow_metrics', and 'percentile_flows'. If not provided, all will be included by default.
schema.title: Include

parameters.description: A comma-separated list of datasets to include in the response. Supported dataset options are: 'analyses_assim', 'forecasts_short_range', 'forecasts_medium_range', 'forecasts_long_range', 'return_periods', 'flow_metrics', and 'percentile_flows'. If not provided, all will be included by default.

parameters.name: reference_time

schema.description: The reference time for the forecast and analysis-assimilation data to include in the response, preferably in ISO format (e.g. '2024-01-01T00:00:00'). If not provided, the most recent forecast reference time will be used by default. This parameter is only relevant if forecast datasets are included in the request.
schema.title: Reference Time

parameters.description: The reference time for the forecast and analysis-assimilation data to include in the response, preferably in ISO format (e.g. '2024-01-01T00:00:00'). If not provided, the most recent forecast reference time will be used by default. This parameter is only relevant if forecast datasets are included in the request.

parameters.name: time_zone

schema.description: The time zone for the provided reference_time and the forecast and analysis-assimilation data included in the response. Default is 'UTC'.
schema.title: Time Zone

parameters.description: The time zone for the provided reference_time and the forecast and analysis-assimilation data included in the response. Default is 'UTC'.

parameters.name: metadata

schema.description: Whether to embed metadata with the actual data.
schema.title: Metadata

parameters.description: Whether to embed metadata with the actual data.
